creator: trasz@FreeBSD.org
text:
First, implementing it the way it was done in iscontrol(8) would be hard, because it would require iscsictl(8) reporting discovery results, while the actual discovery is done by iscsid(8) in the background.  It was done that way, because that's what you usually want: not an "administrative action" performed once, to see what's there, but the ability to connect whatever target you are authorized (by the target) to connect to.  Besides, usually each target has different authentication credentials, so you won't be able to connect to the wrong one.

But yeah, you do have a point.  And I have an idea.  What do you think about a flag that basically says "don't connect to that target"?  You could pass it on iscsictl command line when adding a session, discovery or normal one.  You could see available targets in "iscsictl -L" output, and clear the flag using "iscsictl -M".  What do you think?
